assertions.text: Sections are labeled with time estimates (e.g., [00:00-01:30] HOOK)
The script opens with a hook that creates curiosity or emotional investment within the first 3 sentences
Production notes for the host appear in [square brackets] throughout the script (e.g., [PAUSE], [SLOW DOWN])
The body text reads as continuous spoken prose with no bullet points or headers within the body
Total word count is calibrated to the requested episode duration at 130-150 words per minute
Output includes up to three versions labeled by placement: pre-roll, mid-roll, and/or post-roll
Each version shows approximate word count and duration
The promo code or CTA from the input appears clearly in the ad copy
The ad copy sounds like the host speaking naturally, not like commercial copy
Transition into and out of the ad is written explicitly, not left to the host to improvise
